test_description: For each masked map, subtract the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels, then estimate the low-multipole coefficients a_lm for l=2,3,4,5 using the same masked-sky weighted least-squares fit for the Planck map and all simulations. For each l, form the 3x3 angular-momentum dispersion matrix Q_ij^(l) = Re[sum_mm' a_lm* <lm|L_i L_j|lm'> a_lm'] / [l(l+1) sum_m |a_lm|^2], and take its largest-eigenvalue eigenvector n_l as the preferred axis for that multipole, treating n_l and -n_l as equivalent. Combine the four axes with C = (1/4) sum_{l=2}^5 n_l n_l^T, and define the anomaly statistic as the largest eigenvalue of C. This scalar lies between about 1/3 for mutually dispersed axes and 1 for strongly clustered axes; compare the Planck value to the empirical distribution from the 1000 LCDM simulations processed identically.
test_hypothesis: The Planck low-l angular-momentum preferred axes for ell=2–5 are anomalously concentrated/aligned relative to LCDM simulations, giving an unusually large lambda_max(C).
justificaton: The statistic is explicitly an axis-concentration measure, with larger values indicating stronger clustering of the multipole axes, and the code identifies the upper tail as the tail of interest.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Angular-Momentum Axis Concentration: remove the best-fit monopole and Cartesian dipole on unmasked pixels, estimate ell=2..5 coefficients with a common masked-sky weighted least-squares fit, compute each multipole's angular-momentum-dispersion preferred axis, and return the largest eigenvalue of the mean axis outer-product matrix.
custom_summary.statistic: lambda_max(C), where C = (1/4) sum_{ell=2}^5 n_ell n_ell^T and n_ell is the largest-dispersion angular-momentum axis of multipole ell
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail: larger lambda_max(C) means the ell=2,3,4,5 preferred axes are more mutually clustered
custom_summary.expected_range: approximately 1/3 for dispersed/isotropic axes to 1 for nearly identical axes
custom_summary.monopole_dipole_treatment: best-fit monopole plus Cartesian dipole removed on finite unmasked pixels before low-l coefficient fitting
custom_summary.low_l_coefficient_estimator: simultaneous equal-pixel-weight masked-sky least-squares fit in a real spherical-harmonic basis for all 32 modes with 2 <= ell <= 5
custom_summary.axis_sign_treatment: n and -n are identified by using outer products n n^T

plot_spec.title: Low-l angular-momentum axis concentration
plot_spec.caption: LCDM simulation distribution of the low-l angular-momentum-axis concentration statistic. Larger values indicate stronger clustering of the preferred axes for ell=2,3,4,5; the Planck map is processed with the identical mask, monopole/dipole removal, and masked-sky least-squares fit.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail of the simulation distribution for the ell=2..5 angular-momentum-axis concentration statistic.

test_summary.Description: Angular-momentum-dispersion preferred axes were estimated for Planck and 1000 matched LCDM simulations for ell=2–5 after masked-sky monopole/dipole removal and identical low-l least-squares coefficient fitting. The statistic was lambda_max(C), where C averages the four unsigned axis outer products; larger values mean stronger axis clustering.
test_summary.Results: Planck gave lambda_max(C)=0.6476, compared with simulation mean 0.6286 and standard deviation 0.1020. This is only mildly above the mean, with z≈0.19, percentile ≈60.3, and one-tailed upper p=0.397.
test_summary.Interpretation: The Planck low-l axes are not unusually concentrated relative to the LCDM simulations. The result is far from the upper-tail anomaly threshold and is consistent with ordinary simulation scatter.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Low-l alignments, especially quadrupole-octopole alignment using angular-momentum-dispersion or related axes, are well known in the CMB-anomaly literature, e.g. de Oliveira-Costa et al. and later WMAP/Planck anomaly studies. However, no close literature match was found for this exact ell=2–5 masked-sky C-matrix concentration statistic.
